fields.equipmentInventory: Spear, 2 Daggers, Backpack, Caltrops, Crowbar, 2 flasks of Oil, 10 days of Rations, Rope, Tinderbox, 10 Torches, and Waterskin.
fields.treasureNotes:
Calligrapher's Supplies
Fine Clothes
Book about Thalassic Mythology
Arcane Focus (crystal)
Dungeoneer's Pack

feats.name: Goose: Golden Egg
feats.details: Whenever you make a critical hit, you can take or give a heroic inspiration. Giving can only be done to allies within 30 ft of you.

feats.name: Goose: Corrupted Egg
feats.short: At 1/2 health, DC15 dex save or 2d8 force and 10ft push.
feats.details: When you are equal to or below 1/2th of health, you explode in frustration. Unleashing a burst of energy with a 15 ft radius around you (once per long rest). Those within your vicinity (allies and enemies alike) will need to make a dexterity saving throw (DC 15). Upon failure, they’ll be pushed 10 ft away and take 2D8 force damage. Upon a successful save, they’ll only take half the damage.

feats.name: Amphibious
feats.short: Ability to breathe and move above and below water.
feats.details:
Aeternians have grown gills, webbing between their phalanges, and translucent pearl-like scales which become  
visible underwater; allowing them to breathe and swim underwater no different than on land.

feats.name: Cold Climate Adept
feats.details:
Aeternians have adapted to the cold of the deep, allowing them to become unbothered by cold climate  
temperatures, though they may require more hydration in warmer climates.

feats.name: Bioluminescence
feats.short: 5/15ft of emitting dim light in natural darkness.
feats.details:
Before the Aeternians had discovered how to light their underwater Queendom, they themselves emitted light  in darkness through bioluminescence.  

When in natural darkness, the scales of the Aeternians softly exude a dim light. On land their scales are less  on the surface of their skin; therefore they only emit dim light for 5 ft, while underwater their scales surface,  due to which the Aeternians’ bioluminescence is strengthened to emitting dim light for 15 ft. The  bioluminescence does not shine within magical darkness.  

The bioluminescence effect comes from releasing the stored light within the scales of the Aeternian.  
Generally, 6–8 hours of exposure to a light source is required to store enough light to generate the effect.  As such, in covert situations, Aeternians who do not wish to shed light will ensure to either not be exposed  to a light source, or to fully cover themselves. However, the lack of natural light is regarded as an indication  of shady business; an Aeternian who does not emit bioluminescence is commonly viewed with suspicion  
by others of their kin.

feats.name: Aquatic Affiliation
feats.details: Due to centuries of living below the water surface, the Aeternians have become adept in communing and  handling aquatic beasts.

feats.name: Siren's Call
feats.short: Can cast Siren's Call equal to proficiency bonus.
feats.details:
The geographical change of the Aeternia Queendom came with new forms of danger. However, due to their  brilliant minds and abundant forms of art, they quickly became connected to the Sirens, a species that are  natural denizens of the sea.  

Through them they learned the Siren's Call, a method to warn others while forcing their assailants to move  away from them. They can cast this spell of warding a number of times equal to their proficiency bonus  per long rest.  

Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is their spellcasting ability for this trait (choose when you select this race). -> Charisma

feats.name: Versatile: Cleric Initiate
feats.short: Guidance & Spare the Dying, Healing Word.
feats.details:
Aeternians are as versatile as their surface-born counterparts: humans. Depending on their origin they gain  a matching feat (gain an Origin feat of choice). -> Cleric Initiate

Two Cantrips. You learn two cantrips of your choice from the Cleric, Druid, or Wizard spell list. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this feat's spells (choose when you select this feat). -> Charisma; Guidance & Spare the Dying

Level 1 Spell. Choose a level 1 spell from the same list you selected for this feat's cantrips. You always have that spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a Long Rest. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have. -> Healing Word

Spell Change. Whenever you gain a new level, you can replace one of the spells you chose for this feat with a different spell of the same level from the chosen spell list.

Repeatable. You can take this feat more than once, but you must choose a different spell list each time.

feats.name: Resourceful
feats.short: Gain Heroic Inspiration at Long Rest.
feats.details: Similar to their surface-born counterparts (humans), Aeternians gain Heroic Inspiration whenever they finish  a Long Rest.

feats.name: Innate Sorcery
feats.short: Spell save DC +1 and advantage on attack rolls.
feats.details:
An event in your past left an indelible mark on you, infusing you with simmering magic. As a Bonus Action, you can unleash that magic for 1 minute, during which you gain the following benefits:

The spell save DC of your Sorcerer spells increases by 1.
You have Advantage on the attack rolls of Sorcerer spells you cast.
You can use this feature twice, and you regain all expended uses of it when you finish a Long Rest.

feats.name: Font of Magic
feats.details:
You can tap into the wellspring of magic within yourself. This wellspring is represented by Sorcery Points, which allow you to create a variety of magical effects.

You have 2 Sorcery Points, and you gain more as you reach higher levels, as shown in the Sorcery Points column of the Sorcerer Features table. You can’t have more Sorcery Points than the number shown in the table for your level. You regain all expended Sorcery Points when you finish a Long Rest.

You can use your Sorcery Points to fuel the options below, along with other features, such as Metamagic, that use those points.

Converting Spell Slots to Sorcery Points. You can expend a spell slot to gain a number of Sorcery Points equal to the slot’s level (no action required).

Creating Spell Slots. As a Bonus Action, you can transform unexpended Sorcery Points into one spell slot. The Creating Spell Slots table shows the cost of creating a spell slot of a given level, and it lists the minimum Sorcerer level you must be to create a slot. You can create a spell slot no higher than level 5.

Any spell slot you create with this feature vanishes when you finish a Long Rest.

Creating Spell Slots
Spell Slot Level	Sorcery Point Cost	Min. Sorcerer Level
1	2	2
2	3	3
3	5	5
4	6	7
5	7	9

feats.name: Metamagic
feats.details:
Heightened: Cost: 2 Sorcery Points
When you cast a spell that forces a creature to make a saving throw, you can spend 2 Sorcery Points to give one target of the spell Disadvantage on saves against the spell.

Twinned: Source: Cost: 1 Sorcery Point
When you cast a spell, such as Charm Person, that can be cast with a higher-level spell slot to target an additional creature, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to increase the spell’s effective level by 1.

Because your magic flows from within, you can alter your spells to suit your needs; you gain two Metamagic options of your choice from “Metamagic Options” later in this class’s description. You use the chosen options to temporarily modify spells you cast. To use an option, you must spend the number of Sorcery Points that it costs.

You can use only one Metamagic option on a spell when you cast it unless otherwise noted in one of those options.

Whenever you gain a Sorcerer level, you can replace one of your Metamagic options with one you don’t know. You gain two more options at Sorcerer level 10 and two more at Sorcerer level 17.

spells.name: Guidance
spells.effect: You touch a willing creature and choose a skill. Until the spell ends, the creature adds 1d4 to any ability check using the chosen skill.
spells.details: You touch a willing creature and choose a skill. Until the spell ends, the creature adds 1d4 to any ability check using the chosen skill.

spells.name: Spare the Dying
spells.effect: Choose a creature within range that has 0 Hit Points and isn’t dead. The creature becomes Stable.
spells.details:
Choose a creature within range that has 0 Hit Points and isn’t dead. The creature becomes Stable.

Cantrip Upgrade. The range doubles when you reach levels 5 (30 feet), 11 (60 feet), and 17 (120 feet).

spells.name: Healing Word
spells.effect: A creature of your choice that you can see within range regains Hit Points equal to 2d4 plus your spellcasting ability modifier.
spells.details:
A creature of your choice that you can see within range regains Hit Points equal to 2d4 plus your spellcasting ability modifier.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The healing increases by 2d4 for each spell slot level above 1.

spells.name: Mage Hand
spells.effect: A spectral, floating hand appears at a point you choose within range. The hand lasts for the duration.
spells.details: A spectral, floating hand appears at a point you choose within range. The hand lasts for the duration. The hand vanishes if it is ever more than 30 feet away from you or if you cast this spell again. When you cast the spell, you can use the hand to manipulate an object, open an unlocked door or container, stow or retrieve an item from an open container, or pour the contents out of a vial. As a Magic action on your later turns, you can control the hand thus again. As part of that action, you can move the hand up to 30 feet. The hand can’t attack, activate magic items, or carry more than 10 pounds.

spells.name: Mending
spells.effect: This spell repairs a single break or tear in an object you touch, such as a broken chain link, two halves of a broken key, a torn cloak, or a leaking wineskin.
spells.details: This spell repairs a single break or tear in an object you touch, such as a broken chain link, two halves of a broken key, a torn cloak, or a leaking wineskin. As long as the break or tear is no larger than 1 foot in any dimension, you mend it, leaving no trace of the former damage. This spell can physically repair a magic item, but it can’t restore magic to such an object.

spells.name: Prestidigitation
spells.effect: You create a magical effect within range. Choose the effect from the options below.
spells.details:
You create a magical effect within range. Choose the effect from the options below. If you cast this spell multiple times, you can have up to three of its non-instantaneous effects active at a time.

Sensory Effect. You create an instantaneous, harmless sensory effect, such as a shower of sparks, a puff of wind, faint musical notes, or an odd odor.

Fire Play. You instantaneously light or snuff out a candle, a torch, or a small campfire.

Clean or Soil. You instantaneously clean or soil an object no larger than 1 cubic foot.

Minor Sensation. You chill, warm, or flavor up to 1 cubic foot of nonliving material for 1 hour.

Magic Mark. You make a color, a small mark, or a symbol appear on an object or a surface for 1 hour.

Minor Creation. You create a nonmagical trinket or an illusory image that can fit in your hand. It lasts until the end of your next turn. A trinket can deal no damage and has no monetary worth.

spells.name: Sorcerous Burst
spells.effect: You cast sorcerous energy at one creature or object within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target.
spells.details:
You cast sorcerous energy at one creature or object within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes 1d8 damage of a type you choose: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, Psychic, or Thunder. If you roll an 8 on a d8 for this spell, you can roll another d8, and add it to the damage. When you cast this spell, the maximum number of these d8s you can add to the spell’s damage equals your spellcasting ability modifier.

Cantrip Upgrade. The damage increases by 1d8 when you reach levels 5 (2d8), 11 (3d8), and 17 (4d8).

spells.name: Bubble Spray
spells.effect: Mystifying bubbles fill a 15-foot cone, dealing Psychic damage and briefly blinding creatures that fail a Wisdom save.
spells.details:
You spray a dazzling collection of mystifying bubbles from your hands. Each creature in a 15-foot cone originating from you must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or become blinded until the end of your next turn. In addition, upon a failed save, the creature takes 1d6 Psychic damage, or half as much on a successful save.

Natural fires within the cone are extinguished.

Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 1.

spells.name: Fog Cloud
spells.effect: You create a 20-foot-radius Sphere of fog centered on a point within range.
spells.details:
You create a 20-foot-radius Sphere of fog centered on a point within range. The Sphere is Heavily Obscured. It lasts for the duration or until a strong wind (such as one created by Gust of Wind) disperses it.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The fog’s radius increases by 20 feet for each spell slot level above 1.

spells.name: Siren's Call
spells.effect: A warning cry blasts creatures in a cone for 2d8 Thunder damage and pushes them away on a failed Constitution save.
spells.details:
Through the echoes, your call for help shall be heard. A defensive spell created by the magic researchers of the Aeternia kingdom. As you cry for help, you forcefully push away those who mean you harm. However, be mindful, as sound transfers differently under water.

On Land. Each creature within a 15-foot cone makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, they take 2d8 Thunder damage and are pushed away from you by 5 feet. On a successful save, they take half the damage and are not pushed. Your call travels over 100 feet on land.

Under Water. Each creature within a 20-foot cone makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, they take 2d8 Thunder damage and are pushed away from you by 15 feet. On a successful save, they take half the damage and are not pushed. Your call travels over 300 feet through water.

Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 1.

spells.name: Whirlpool
spells.effect: Release a whirlpool in a 5-foot square: it deals Cold damage and knocks a target prone or drives it downward under water.
spells.details:
As you spin around your vial of water, a whirlpool comes to be. Uncorking the vial releases that whirlpool upon a target within range. The target must make a Dexterity saving throw or experience the full effect of the spell. The effect differs depending on whether the target is on land or below the surface.

On Land. The target takes 3d8 Cold damage and falls prone. On a successful save, the target takes half the damage and does not fall prone.

Under Water. The target takes 3d10 Cold damage and is pushed downward by 30 feet. On a successful save, the target takes half the damage and is not pushed downward.

Higher-Level Spell Slot. For each spell slot level above 1, an extra whirlpool is created.
spells.componentsText: S, M (a small vial of water)

spells.name: Mage Armor
spells.effect: You touch a willing creature who isn’t wearing armor. Until the spell ends, the target’s base AC becomes 13 plus its Dexterity modifier.
spells.details: You touch a willing creature who isn’t wearing armor. Until the spell ends, the target’s base AC becomes 13 plus its Dexterity modifier. The spell ends early if the target dons armor.
spells.componentsText: V, S, M (a piece of cured leather)

inventoryItems.name: Arcane Focus (crystal)
inventoryItems.details:
Adventuring Gear · Weight: Varies

An Arcane Focus takes one of the forms in the Arcane Focuses table and is bejeweled or carved to channel arcane magic. A Sorcerer, Warlock, or Wizard can use such an item as a Spellcasting Focus. Focus Weight Cost Crystal 1 lb. 10 GP Orb 3 lb. 20 GP Rod 2 lb. 10 GP Staff (also a Quarterstaff) 4 lb. 5 GP Wand 1 lb. 10 GP

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Backpack
inventoryItems.details:
Adventuring Gear · Weight: 5 lb.

A Backpack holds up to 30 pounds within 1 cubic foot. It can also serve as a saddlebag.

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Bead of Nourishment
inventoryItems.details:
Magic Item · Common

Wondrous Item, Common

This flavorless, gelatinous bead dissolves on your tongue and provides as much nourishment as 1 day of Rations.

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Calligrapher's Supplies
inventoryItems.details:
Tool

Ability: Dexterity Weight: 5 lb. Utilize: Write text with impressive flourishes that guard against forgery (DC 15) Craft: Ink, Spell Scroll

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Caltrops
inventoryItems.details:
Adventuring Gear · Weight: 2 lb.

As a Utilize action, you can spread Caltrops from their bag to cover a 5-footsquare area within 5 feet of yourself. A creature that enters this area for the first time on a turn must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or take 1 Piercing damage and have its Speed reduced to 0 until the start of its next turn. It takes 10 minutes to recover the Caltrops.

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Crowbar
inventoryItems.details:
Adventuring Gear · Weight: 5 lb.

Using a Crowbar gives you Advantage on Strength checks where the Crowbar’s leverage can be applied.

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Dagger
inventoryItems.details:
Weapon · Weight: 1 lb.

1d4 Piercing; Finesse, Light, Thrown (Range 20/60); Nick

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Fine Clothes
inventoryItems.details:
Adventuring Gear · Weight: 6 lb.

Fine Clothes are made of expensive fabrics and adorned with expertly crafted details. Some events and locations admit only people wearing these clothes.

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Flasks of Oil
inventoryItems.details:
Adventuring Gear · Weight: 1 lb.

You can douse a creature, object, or space with Oil or use it as fuel, as detailed below. Dousing a Creature or an Object. When you take the Attack action, you can replace one of your attacks with throwing an Oil flask. Target one creature or object within 20 feet of yourself. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Dexterity modifier and Proficiency Bonus) or be covered in oil. If the target takes Fire damage before the oil dries (after 1 minute), the target takes an extra 5 Fire damage from burning oil. Dousing a Space. You can take the Utilize action to pour an Oil flask on level ground to cover a 5-footsquare area within 5 feet of yourself. If lit, the oil burns until the end of the turn 2 rounds from when the oil was lit (or 12 seconds) and deals 5 Fire damage to any creature that enters the area or ends its turn there. A creature can take this damage only once per turn. 99 Fuel. Oil serves as fuel for Lamps and Lanterns. Once lit, a flask of Oil burns for 6 hours in a Lamp or Lantern. That duration doesn’t need to be consecutive; you can extinguish the burning Oil (as a Utilize action) and rekindle it again until it has burned for a total of 6 hours.

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Rations
inventoryItems.details:
Adventuring Gear · Weight: 2 lb.

Rations consist of travelready food, including jerky, dried fruit, hardtack, and nuts. See “Malnutrition” in “Rules Glossary” for the risks of not eating.

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Rope
inventoryItems.details:
Adventuring Gear · Weight: 5 lb.

As a Utilize action, you can tie a knot with Rope if you succeed on a DC 10 Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check. The Rope can be burst with a successful DC 20 Strength (Athletics) check. You can bind an unwilling creature with the Rope only if the creature has the Grappled, Incapacitated, or Restrained condition. If the creature’s legs are bound, the creature has the Restrained condition until it escapes. Escaping the Rope requires the creature to make a successful DC 15 Dexterity (Acrobatics) check as an action.

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Spear
inventoryItems.details:
Weapon · Weight: 3 lb.

1d6 Piercing; Thrown (Range 20/60), Versatile (1d8); Sap

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Tinderbox
inventoryItems.details:
Adventuring Gear · Weight: 1 lb.

A Tinderbox is a small container holding flint, fire steel, and tinder (usually dry cloth soaked in light oil) used to kindle a fire. Using it to light a Candle, Lamp, Lantern, or Torch—or anything else with exposed fuel—takes a Bonus Action. Lighting any other fire takes 1 minute.

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Torches
inventoryItems.details:
Adventuring Gear · Weight: 1 lb.

A Torch burns for 1 hour, casting Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 20 feet. When you take the Attack action, you can attack with the Torch, using it as a Simple Melee weapon. On a hit, the target takes 1 Fire damage.

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Waterskin
inventoryItems.details:
Adventuring Gear · Weight: 5 lb. (full)

A Waterskin holds up to 4 pints. If you don’t drink sufficient water, you risk dehydration (see “Rules Glossary”). A mount can help you move more quickly through the wilderness, but its primary purpose is to carry gear that would otherwise slow you down. The Mounts and Other Animals table shows each animal’s carrying capacity. See “Monsters” for the animals’ stat blocks. An animal pulling a carriage, cart, chariot, sled, or wagon can move weight up to five times its base carrying capacity, including the weight of the vehicle. If multiple animals pull the same vehicle, add their carrying capacities together. Barding is armor designed for a mount. Any type of armor on the Armor table in “Equipment” can be purchased as barding. The cost is four times the normal cost, and it weighs twice as much. A saddle comes with a bit, a bridle, reins, and any other equipment needed to use the saddle. A Military Saddle gives Advantage on any ability check you make to remain mounted. An Exotic Saddle is required for riding an aquatic or a flying mount. Mounts and Other Animals Item Carrying Capacity Cost Camel 450 lb. 50 GP Elephant 1,320 lb. 200 GP Horse, Draft 540 lb. 50 GP Horse, Riding 480 lb. 75 GP Mastiff 195 lb. 25 GP Mule 420 lb. 8 GP Pony 225 lb. 30 GP Warhorse 540 lb. 400 GP Item Weight Cost Carriage 600 lb. 100 GP Cart 200 lb. 15 GP Chariot 100 lb. 250 GP Feed per day 10 lb. 5 CP Saddle Exotic 40 lb. 60 GP Military 30 lb. 20 GP Riding 25 lb. 10 GP Sled 300 lb. 20 GP Stabling per day — 5 SP Wagon 400 lb. 35 GP The Airborne and Waterborne Vehicles table provides statistics for various types of large vehicles. The following notes apply. A ship sailing against a strong wind moves at half speed. In a dead calm (no wind), waterborne ships can’t move under sail and must be rowed. Keelboats and Rowboats are used on lakes and rivers. If going downstream, add the speed of the current (typically 3 miles per hour) to the speed of the vehicle. These vehicles can’t be rowed against any significant current, but they can be pulled upstream by draft animals on the shores. A Rowboat can be carried and weighs 100 pounds.

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryEquipment.name: Arcane Focus (crystal)
inventoryEquipment.details:
Adventuring Gear · Weight: Varies

An Arcane Focus takes one of the forms in the Arcane Focuses table and is bejeweled or carved to channel arcane magic. A Sorcerer, Warlock, or Wizard can use such an item as a Spellcasting Focus. Source: SRD 5.2.1

consumables.name: Flasks of Oil
consumables.details:
Adventuring Gear · Weight: 1 lb.

You can douse a creature, object, or space with Oil or use it as fuel, as detailed below. Dousing a Creature or an Object. When you take the Attack action, you can replace one of your attacks with throwing an Oil flask. Target one creature or object within 20 feet of yourself. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Dexterity modifier and Proficiency Bonus) or be covered in oil. If the target takes Fire damage before the oil dries (after 1 minute), the target takes an extra 5 Fire damage from burning oil. Dousing a Space. You can take the Utilize action to pour an Oil flask on level ground to cover a 5-footsquare area within 5 feet of yourself. If lit, the oil burns until the end of the turn 2 rounds from when the oil was lit (or 12 seconds) and deals 5 Fire damage to any creature that enters the area or ends its turn there. A creature can take this damage only once per turn. 99 Fuel. Oil serves as fuel for Lamps and Lanterns. Once lit, a flask of Oil burns for 6 hours in a Lamp or Lantern. That duration doesn’t need to be consecutive; you can extinguish the burning Oil (as a Utilize action) and rekindle it again until it has burned for a total of 6 hours.

Source: SRD 5.2.1

consumables.name: Rations
consumables.details:
Adventuring Gear · Weight: 2 lb.

Rations consist of travelready food, including jerky, dried fruit, hardtack, and nuts. See “Malnutrition” in “Rules Glossary” for the risks of not eating.

Source: SRD 5.2.1
